title: Cozy river mystery
text: Small town secrets, soft danger, and justice that prefers truth over body counts.

title: Heist crew clockwork
text: Plans, roles, and timers; greed is a character, not just a motive.

title: Council vote knives
text: Every alliance is temporary; information is the sharpest inventory item.

title: Slice-of-life stakes
text: Jobs, festivals, and relationships drive progress; monsters are rare interruptions.

title: Casefile grind
text: Clues, dead ends, and witnesses who edit themselves; progress is a board of strings.

title: School arc pressures
text: Classes, clubs, exams, and social ladders; the dungeon is the timetable.

title: Market of seven smells
text: A market where gossip travels faster than goods; every stall has a second ledger.

title: Opera box politics
text: Private boxes where deals finish before the aria; ushers sell silence.

title: After-hours library
text: Stacks open to members with keys and blackmail; books leave footprints.

title: Vault-row understreet
text: A street of private vaults; locks are art, guards are critics.

title: Cafe of quiet networks
text: A cafe where tables are territories and overheard talk is inventory.
examples: table three is free if you tip secrets

title: Ink press club
text: Writers who print truth for sale and fiction for free samples.

title: Midnight mask society
text: A club where faces are optional and introductions are riddles.

title: Specialist heist table
text: Specialists who hate each other professionally and need each other practically.

title: Festival parent council
text: A council that runs festivals and quietly runs the town.

title: Noisy-room lip skim
text: Catch three words across a loud room; wrong lighting makes poetry of nonsense.
examples: catch the price, miss the name

title: Lock-click ear
text: Hear pin stacks through thin doors; thick vaults hum lies.

title: Alibi clock sense
text: Notice when a story timeline does not fit walking distances in this city.

title: Crowd-fade posture
text: Stand like furniture in a crowd; works until someone looks for you specifically.

title: Rumor-seed plant
text: Plant a short rumor that travels one social hop; farther hops mutate it.
examples: hop one is clean, hop three is wild

title: Wry warmth
text: Humor that cares; danger exists but people still share bread and jokes.

title: Tight timer tension
text: Scenes feel like countdowns; waste is a villain.

title: Wrong invitation in hand
text: You arrive to a party you were not meant to attend; coat check knows your face anyway.

title: Job briefing on a napkin
text: A heist briefing drawn on a napkin with coffee rings as walls.

title: Club signup day chaos
text: Signup tables, rival clubs, and a form that asks for skills you do not have yet.
